title: [hlstool] Add hlstool for composing high-level abstractions and passes
body:
... the long awaited.

This intends to be a mostly boilerplate-y commit for introducing an `hlstool` to circt. The goal is fairly clear - provide a tool which composes the various passes and abstractions in CIRCT which care about high-level design/synthesis. By doing so, the tool intends to take out the guesswork in terms of how these abstractions are to be composed, as well as when and where to run a pass. Tool "boilerplate" is based on `firtool`. For now, a non-parameterized Handshake flow is implemented. Follow up commit should extend the tool with static flows, more handshake parameterization, scheduling, ...

Benefits should be fairly immediate - both in terms of reducing the verbosity of integration tests (many of which currently have to manually describe a fairly long pass pipeline) as well as for newcomers to CIRCT, which can be pointed to a tool/pass pipeline that shows a verified phase ordering of lowering/transformation passes.

As for tests, this commit includes modifications to most of the Handshake integration tests using the 'all' driver. To replace all tests, a follow-up commit should define how to handle flow-specific arguments to `hlstool`.

Addresses #3864.

Q: Currently the tool has a notion of input- and output IR abstraction level (https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/3866/files#diff-691fcc7325d249227a5380b4f77f5d737aae576220003c4c84a010320499a143R112-R122) to easily select which part of the overall pass pipeline to run - this is to facilitate testing.
Integers are chosen as IR abstraction levels and it is up to the user to know which value corresponds to which abstraction in the flow. This simplifies the code a bit, but i fear that it might be too tribal for newcomers - do you think going string-based here be more appropriate?
